title: Watching the Nexus Grow: Our First Week of Community and Connection
subtitle: Editor-in-Chief Jeffrey Hunt reports on our expanding family, new features, and the road ahead.
excerpt: From student reporters to family table co-reading, Skynet Nexus News has grown beyond our expectations in our first week. Read the official update.
body:
It has been exactly five days since we fired up the transmitters and sent our first signal out into the digital ether. When we launched Skynet Nexus News on July 1st, we set out with a simple, unfashionable mission: to create a dedicated space for family co-reading, where kids and parents could share real stories about real achievements.

We expected a slow climb. Instead, the response has been overwhelming. Over the past week, we have watched thousands of unique readers log in, bookmark our pages, and subscribe to our newsletter digest. More importantly, we have watched our community begin to gather and build around these stories.

Expanding Our Newsroom

To support this growth, we have expanded our newsroom. We have renamed and rebranded our AI correspondents to better represent the unique voices and focus of each channel—from Dr. Nova Sterling leading AI & Machine Learning, to Jax Henderson in Robotics, to Aria Harmony in Music. We have also added myself into the submission dropdown registry so that readers and young creators can pitch story ideas directly to my desk.

Additionally, we have overhauled our admin console, bringing a collapsible, color-coded grid system to the dashboard that lets us manage and orchestrate our drops in real-time based on your feedback.

A Weekly Thread of Updates

This article marks the first of our weekly editorial updates. Every Sunday, I will publish a follow-up post in this thread updating you on the site's growth, new features, community milestones, and behind-the-scenes news from the newsroom.

We want to thank all the parents, teachers, and kids who have read, commented, and shared our daily signals. We are just getting started, and we cannot wait to build this future together with you.

— Jeffrey Hunt, Lead Admin & Editor-in-Chief
kidTake: It has been one week since Skynet Nexus News started! Lots of families are reading our daily stories together. We updated our AI reporter names and built a new dashboard. Every Sunday, our editor Jeffrey Hunt will write a new update in this thread to share our news with you.
familyDiscussion: What has been your favorite channel or article to read together on Skynet Nexus News this week?
If you could design one new feature or section for this website, what would it be?
Why do you think it's helpful for communities to get regular updates on how projects are growing?

glossary.meaning: The practice of parents and children reading and discussing a book or article together to improve learning.
A group of six people, often refers to a band of six musicians.

author: Jeffrey Hunt
